data.title: Asian Stock Markets Fall on Tariff ConcernsUpdate
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Asian equity markets declined on Wednesday, following U.S. stock futures lower as caution prevailed, with investors awaiting further clarity on the Trump administration's trade policies while continuing to parse the fast-moving tariff developments.

The benchmark Hang Seng Index was down 2.5% by midday in Hong Kong, led by consumer-oriented internet stocks like Meituan and JD.com, which dropped 7.4% and 6.2%, respectively. China's Shanghai Composite Index was 0.9% lower.

Japan's Nikkei Stock Average shed 0.9%. The Kospi of South Korea, another export-driven and tech-heavy economy, fell 0.6%.

Chip and electronic stocks were among the biggest losers on both indexes. South Korean memory-chip maker SK Hynix, which supplies high-bandwidth-memory products to Nvidia, dropped 3.0%, tracking Nvidia's after-hours decline. Samsung Electronics was down 2.8%. In Japan, Advantest lost 4.8% and Nidec was 3.4% lower.

Nvidia said in a filing that it anticipates a charge of up to $5.5 billion tied to exporting its artificial-intelligence chips to China, after the U.S. government told the company that it will implement a license requirement to export its AI H20 chips to China and five other nations.

"The Street will take this news with clear nervousness worried these are the first shots fired in the tech battle between the U.S. and China," analysts from Wedbush Securities wrote in a note. "Beijing/Xi are not just going to take this news and walk away."

Technology companies listed in Hong Kong were also lower. Alibaba Group lost 5.0% and Tencent was off by 3.2%.

The declines came despite China's first-quarter economic growth beating expectations. Official data showed that the country's gross domestic product growth rate held steady at 5.4%, topping the 5.2% rise tipped by a Wall Street Journal survey of economists.

In another sign of market nerves, spot gold surpassed $3,280 an ounce to hit a new intraday high.

Trepidation over tit-for-tat tariffs remained at the front of investors' minds after President Trump said Tuesday that China will need to come to the U.S. if it wants to negotiate for lower tariffs.

"The ball is in China's court," the president said in a new statement read at the White House press briefing by press secretary Karoline Leavitt. "China needs to make a deal with us, we don't have to make a deal with them."

Earlier this week, the Trump administration paused tariffs on some consumer electronics, giving investors a brief reprieve. Stocks broadly advanced across the region, though the cloud of uncertainty surrounding U.S. tariff policy continued to hang over market sentiment.

Asian currencies were mixed Wednesday. The safe-haven yen strengthened to around 142.60 to the U.S. dollar. The dollar rose slightly against the Korean won, while the Australian dollar was steady against its U.S. counterpart.

Morgan Stanley in a new report cut its December targets for several Asian stock indexes after lowering growth forecasts for global and Asia and emerging markets, revising foreign-exchange projections and considering other variables like oil prices.

"We see little upside from current spot levels across our coverage in the base case, with risks skewed to the downside in the near term," the investment bank's research team said.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Yangzijiang Shipbuilding Buys Back SG$4 Million Worth of Shares
data.description:
Yangzijiang Shipbuilding bought back 2 million shares in the open market on Tuesday for SG$4 million, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The shipbuilder has been authorized to buy back around 395.1 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 13 million shares.

data.title: Finland's Nordea tops earnings estimates in 1st quarter
data.description: Nordea Bank reported first-quarter operating earnings above market estimates on Wednesday, as higher income and lower expenses and loan losses compared to the previous quarter boosted the Nordic region's biggest lender.

data.title: Hongkong Post Halts U.S.-Bound Parcel Services Amid Escalating Trade Tensions
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Hong Kong's postal service is halting shipments bound for the U.S., citing what it described as the Trump administration's "unreasonable" trade policy.

"The U.S. is unreasonable, bullying and imposing tariffs abusively," Hongkong Post said in a statement on Wednesday.

The postal service provider said its decision was due to the U.S.'s elimination of duty-free "de minimis" treatment for low-value parcels from China and Hong Kong, as well as imposing tariffs on dozens of countries.

President Trump signed an executive order earlier this month scrapping de minimis exemption for low-value parcels from China. The measure had allowed businesses like Shein and Temu to avoid import taxes on shipments with a retail value of $800 or less. The order, which takes effect on May 2, means Chinese e-commerce companies will no longer benefit from the exemption.

The U.S. Postal Service briefly stopped accepting parcels from mainland China and Hong Kong in February amid confusion over the cancellation of the de minimis exemption, before resuming acceptance.

"Hongkong Post will definitely not collect any so-called tariffs on behalf of the U.S. and will suspend the acceptance of postal items containing goods destined to the U.S.," it added.

Non-air transport of parcels bound for the U.S. has been halted immediately, and airmail services will cease from April 27, Hongkong Post said. Mail containing only documents will not be affected.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: Australia's NeuroScientific Biopharmaceuticals soars on buying biotech co
data.description:
** NeuroScientific Biopharmaceuticals surges 77.1% to A$0.062, highest since April 11, 2024

** Pharma firm says it will buy private co Isopogen WA for its patented stem cell tech to manufacture cellular medicine mesenchymal stromal cells

** Raises A$3.5 mln ($2.2 mln) at issue price of A$0.035

** Stock sees busiest session since April 2024, with about 8.2 mln shares traded - 96.6x the 30-day avg

** NSB up 78.8% YTD

($1 = A$1.5773)

data.title: Hyterra to Begin Drilling Works at Nemaha Project in Kansas; Shares Fall 3%
data.description:
Hyterra is set to conduct drilling activities at the Nemaha project in Kansas, US, with the Murfin rig 116 already at the Sue Duroche 3 well site, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company plans to drill both the sedimentary and Precambrian basement sections and confirm the presence and concentrations of hydrogen and helium, the filing stated. It also aims to assess the quality of the reservoir rocks.

HyTerra has a 12-month program that aims to explore the potential of natural white hydrogen in Kansas via its subsidiary, HYT Operating LLC, the filing stated.

The natural gas explorer saw its shares down 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Crimson Tide Names New Interim Executive Chair
data.description:
Crimson Tide named Barrie Whipp as the new interim executive chair, effective immediately.

Whipp succeeded Jacqueline Daniell, who stepped down, the software company said Tuesday.

data.title: Hazer Target Price Raised 12% to A$0.64/Share by Euroz Hartleys>HZR.AU
India's Gensol Engineering sinks as regulator takes action after debt defaults
data.description:
** Shares of Gensol Engineering down 5%, the maximum drop allowed by the exchange, at 122.68 rupees

** Indian market regulator restricts co's founders from holding any key position at any listed co until further orders, for diversion of funds and fraudulent practices, and debt defaults

** Regulator also bars co's founders from the securities market,

** Further, bars the construction firm from conducting a 1:10 stock split approved on Saturday

** In March and April, GENO shares have fallen in all but two trading sessions

** GENO shares have fallen nearly 71% since March 3, when Care Edge significantly downgraded the firm's credit rating, citing ongoing debt repayment delays

data.title: MIDEAST - Factors to watch - April 16
data.description:
Here are some factors that may affect Middle East stock markets on Wednesday. Reuters has not verified the press reports and does not vouch for their accuracy.

INTERNATIONAL/REGIONAL

* GLOBAL MARKETS-Shares fall in Asia as Nvidia curbs fan worries, gold hits record

* Oil dips as markets assess trade war effects

* PRECIOUS-Safe-haven gold hits record high on weaker dollar, trade war concerns

* MIDEAST STOCKS-Most Gulf markets mixed amid US tariff uncertainity

* World oil demand, U.S. supply to grow more slowly on tariff tensions, IEA says

* Rape used systematically as a weapon of war in Sudan, UN agency warns

SAUDI ARABIA

* UCIC to float 30% stake in first Saudi IPO since tariff-driven sell-off

KUWAIT

* Kuwait commences merger of state oil firms KNPC and KIPIC, Kuna reports

Seatrium Buys Back Shares Worth SG$4.6 Million
data.description:
Seatrium (SGX:S51) repurchased 2.6 million shares in the open market on Tuesday for SG$4.6 million, or SG$1.7801 apiece, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The ship repair company has been authorized to buy back 68.2 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back around 30.0 million shares.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.1% to 76,822, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 rose 0.1% to 23,354, with investors acutely sensitive to further developments in U.S. tariff policies.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.2% versus the U.S. dollar to 85.57, aided by broad weakness in the dollar and mild inflows.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 102.625 rupees, with the yield up slightly at 6.4155% even as underlying sentiment remained positive after drop in inflation.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was flat at 5.7475%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was flat at 5.705%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.90%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.76%

data.title: Envipco Secures Reverse Vending Machines Order from Romanian Retail Group
data.description:
Envipco secured an order to supply 400 Optima reverse vending machines to an unnamed retail group in Romania.

The Netherlands-based reverse vending machine technology company will deliver the equipment through 2025, according to a Tuesday release.

data.title: Nordea: Households Are Focusing on Saving, Corporates Strengthening Their Balance Sheets
Nordea Expects Full-Year Cost Growth of 2.0%-2.5%
Power and Instrumentation (Gujarat) Bags INR802 Million Work Order
data.description:
Power and Instrumentation (Gujarat) has secured a work order worth 802.4 million Indian rupees from Godrej & Boyce Mfg. Co., according to a BSE filing on Tuesday.

The contract entails the construction of 350 MVA power transformers, oil filtration, pre-erection testing, and oil sample testing at the 400kV GIS PSS-2 GIPCL, Khavda Project in India.

The project marks the company's entry into the extra high voltage segment.

data.title: Ampol Q1 Sales Volume Drops, Refiner Margins Trigger Potential Fuel Security Payment
data.description:
Ampol said its total sales volume declined 7.4% in the first quarter to 6.1 million liters from 6.6 million liters a year ago, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The company reported mid-single-digit percentage growth in earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) from each of its convenience retail segments and its New Zealand segment.

The company said its first-quarter Lytton refiner margin was $6.07 per barrel with a total production of 1.3 million liters including the impact of Cyclone Alfred.

The company reported that refiner margins in March and early April have dropped to levels that, if sustained throughout the second fiscal quarter, would qualify it for payment under the Fuel Security Services Payment program providing downside protection during a period of global refining market weakness.

data.title: Australia's Rio Tinto slips on Q1 fall in iron ore shipments to multi-year low, bleak forecast
data.description:
** Shares of Rio Tinto fall 2.3% to A$108.9, on track for weakest session since April 9

** Top miner reports its lowest Q1 iron ore shipments since 2019

** Co expects 2025 iron ore shipments from its flagship Pilbara ops to be at lower end of forecast range of 323 million to 338 million metric tons

** "Pilbara iron ore shipments are expected to be at the lower end of guidance due to the losses sustained from the four cyclones in Q1" - co

** Stock down 7.3% YTD, including moves in current session, compared to a 1.1% YTD decline in the mining sub-index

data.title: Diamyd Medical Receives Australian Patent for Autoimmune Diabetes Treatment
data.description:
Diamyd Medical (DMYD-B.ST) received a patent from the Australian Patent Office for its novel immunotherapeutic composition for the treatment and prevention of autoimmune diabetes.

The patent covers a composition of the antigens glutamic acid decarboxylase and proinsulin immobilized on separate carrier particles, according to a Tuesday release.

The biotechnology company said the patent is valid until 2035.

data.title: India's ICICI Prudential jumps after profit rise; slowdown fears prompt TP cuts
data.description:
** Shares of India's ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company jump 5.4% to 597 rupees

** Co reported a higher quarterly profit on strong group insurance demand

** Brokerage Nuvama says results "in line"; upgrades ICIR to "buy" from "hold" on ~5% stock drop since the start of April

** Antique says valuations factoring concerns of lower growth and is attractive; maintains "buy" but trims TP by 6% to 650 rupees

** Nomura cuts TP from 715 rupees to 650 rupees, maintains neutral and says "no positive catalyst in sight"

** Of 32 analysts covering the stock, at least 16 cut TPs after results while at least six downgraded ICIR - LSEG data

** Stock has fallen 9%, YTD

data.title: United Overseas Bank Buys Back, Cancels Shares Worth Nearly SG$12 Million
data.description:
United Overseas Bank repurchased 350,000 shares in the open market on Tuesday for SG$11.8 million, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The Singapore-based lender canceled 300,000 shares while holding 50,000 shares as treasury stock.

The bank has been authorized to buy back about 83.7 million shares from its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 5.6 million shares.

data.title: Bank of Queensland hits 2-week high on HY profit rise, positive outlook
data.description:
** Bank of Queensland rises as much as 5.9% to A$6.880, highest since April 2

** Lender reports H1 statutory net profit after tax (NPAT) of A$171 mln ($108.3 mln), a 13% y/y rise

** BOQ expects "step up" in H2 headline margin

** Analysts at UBS say bank's cash NPAT results are in line with estimates, with improvements in net interest margin

** Brokerage sees BOQ benefit from portfolio mix changes and digital rollout

** UBS maintains "sell" rating, A$6.50 price target

** Co to pay interim dividend of 18 AU cents/share

** BOQ down ~3% YTD

($1 = A$1.5793)

data.title: African Markets - Factors to watch on April 16
data.description:
The following company announcements, scheduled economic indicators, debt and currency market moves and political events may affect African markets on Wednesday.

- - - - -

GLOBAL MARKETS

Shares fell in Asia on Wednesday as AI darling Nvidia took a hit from U.S. curbs on chip sales to China, highlighting the damage to come in a tit-for-tat global trade war, while gold hit a record and the dollar stayed under pressure.

WORLD OIL PRICES

Oil prices edged lower on Wednesday, as shifting U.S. tariff policies fuelled uncertainty, prompting traders to weigh the potential impact of the U.S.-China trade war on economic growth and energy demand.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

AFRICA STOCKS

For the latest news on South African stocks, click on

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

The South African rand retreated on Tuesday after the country's central bank warned in a biannual review that the scope for monetary policy easing had narrowed because of global economic uncertainty.

NIGERIA INFLATION

Nigeria's headline consumer inflation (NGCPIY=ECI) edged up to 24.23% year-on-year in March from 23.18% in February, its statistics agency said on Tuesday.

NIGERIA PETROLEUM

Nigeria plans to establish a national strategic petroleum products stockpile this year to safeguard its economy against disruptions in the international market, the petroleum products regulator said at a press briefing on Tuesday.

KENYA MARKETS

Kenya's shilling was little changed against the U.S. dollar on Tuesday, data from the London Stock Exchange Group showed.

TULLOW OIL KENYA

West Africa-focused oil and gas explorer Tullow Oil said on Tuesday it would sell its assets in Kenya to Gulf Energy Ltd for at least $120 million as it works to reduce its debt.

TANZANIA POLITICS

Tanzania's main opposition party CHADEMA said on Tuesday its disqualification from elections due later this year was unconstitutional, days after its leader was arrested and charged with treason.

AFRICA CRUDE OIL

A steep drop in crude oil prices largely due to U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs will squeeze budgets of emerging market oil exporters, analysts said, while the potential economic slowdown could also curb any benefits for importers.

BARRICK GOLD MALI

Malian authorities have shut the Canadian miner Barrick Gold's office in the capital Bamako over the alleged non-payment of taxes, two sources close to the matter told Reuters, in the latest escalation of a protracted dispute over mining revenues.

data.title: China appoints new top trade negotiator amid US tariff war
data.description:
China appointed as its new trade negotiator on Wednesday a former representative to the World Trade Organization who replaces Vice Commerce Minister Wang Shouwen, amid an escalating tariff war with the United States.

Li Chenggang, 58, a former assistant commerce minister during the first administration of U.S. President Donald Trump, takes over from Wang, 59, the human resources and social security ministry said in a statement.

The change comes as Beijing pursues a hardline stance in an intensifying trade war with Washington triggered by Trump's hefty tariffs on items imported from China.

Li, who has held several key jobs in the commerce ministry, such as in departments overseeing treaties and law and fair trade, has an academic background in the elite Peking University and Germany's Hamburg University.

He replaces Wang, a veteran commerce official and top trade negotiator since 2022.

In the leadup to the U.S. tariff escalation, Wang welcomed foreign executives in Beijing, some from PepsiCo, Visa, P&G, Rio Tinto and Vale, reassuring them of China's economic prospects.

The step came after official data showed foreign direct investment plummeted 27.1% in local currency terms in 2024 on the year, for its largest such drop since the 2008 global financial crisis.

data.title: Nordea 1Q Common Equity Tier 1 Ratio 15.7% Vs 17.2%
India Car Sales Up 3.7% in March
data.description:
Total passenger vehicle sales in India increased 3.7% year-on-year to 329,742 units in March 2025, matching the growth rate seen in the previous month, according to data from the Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers (SIAM).

The figures exclude sales from BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Jaguar Land Rover (JLR), and Volvo.

This marked the sixth consecutive month of year-on-year growth in passenger vehicle sales.

However, on a monthly basis, sales edged down by 0.5%, following a 5.7% decline in February.

For the first quarter of 2025, total passenger vehicle sales increased 2.4% from a year earlier, reaching 1,162,560 units.
